meta.title: Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine — Duratorq diesel. X400 generation, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 15+ vetted Jaguar specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Duratorq engine typically costs £1,200–£2,500 supply only. Fitting adds £600–£1,200. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Duratorq is rated ★★★☆☆ — it's more reliable than its reputation suggests, but it has known issues: EGR valve clogging and downpipe splitting (the most common), turbo actuator seizure, fuel pump failure, and electrical faults. Regular maintenance is essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d?

acceptedAnswer.text: EGR valve clogging, EGR downpipe splitting, turbo actuator seizure, fuel pump failure, and electrical gremlins. Rust on the rear suspension and subframe is also a major concern.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and body condition. Against an X‑Type worth £1,000–£3,000, a £1,800 recon is a significant investment. Assess the body for rust — if the body is solid, replacement can be worthwhile.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 2.0d uses Ford Duratorq codes FMBA, FMBB or AJ20D4. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 2.0d's EGR system, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — EGR cleaning or replacement is a straightforward repair costing £100–£400. The EGR downpipe is also prone to splitting and can be replaced. Neither issue typically requires engine replacement.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace with upgraded part; common failure point
Free or replace actuator; catch it before turbo damage
Replace pump; regular filter changes prevent recurrence
Assess carefully; often the car's bigger problem

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X‑Type 2.0d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes — the Duratorq is robust when maintained
* Evidence of EGR cleaning or replacement — this is the number one issue
* Good bodywork — rust is a bigger problem than the engine on many X‑Types
avoidIf: * EGR issues (hesitation, flat spots) with no diagnosis
* Engine cutting out — potential fuel pump failure
* Heavy rust on the rear suspension and subframe — this can be uneconomical to repair
* Poor service history — the X‑Type is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X‑Type 2.0d's Duratorq engine is a robust Ford‑sourced unit that's more reliable than its reputation suggests — and far more reliable than the later Ingenium diesels. It's a 2000s diesel with 2000s diesel problems: EGR clogging, turbo actuator issues, and the occasional fuel pump failure. But the real enemy of the X‑Type isn't the engine — it's rust. Buy on bodywork, not on engine size.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * EGR System — Any hesitation or flat spots? EGR valve and downpipe are the number one issue
* Service History — Including which engine era (Duratorq is the only 2.0d engine — verify this before purchase)
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lack of power or black smoke? Turbo actuator may be seized
* Fuel System — Any cutting out or hard starting? Fuel pump failure is a known issue
* Rust — Check rear suspension and subframe — rust is a regular problem on X‑Types
* Electrical System — Any intermittent faults? Electrical gremlins are common
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved EGR, turbo, or fuel issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures; often point to EGR or turbo problems

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned Duratorq engine typically costs £1,200–£2,500 supply only. Fitting adds £600–£1,200. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.
items.value: 2. Is the Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine reliable?

items.label: The Duratorq is rated ★★★☆☆ — it's more reliable than its reputation suggests, but it has known issues: EGR valve clogging and downpipe splitting (the most common), turbo actuator seizure, fuel pump failure, and electrical faults. Regular maintenance is essential.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d?

items.label: EGR valve clogging, EGR downpipe splitting, turbo actuator seizure, fuel pump failure, and electrical gremlins. Rust on the rear suspension and subframe is also a major concern.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on vehicle value and body condition. Against an X‑Type worth £1,000–£3,000, a £1,800 recon is a significant investment. Assess the body for rust — the engine is the least of your worries. If the body is solid, replacement can be worthwhile.
items.value: 5. How do I find my Jaguar X‑Type 2.0d engine code?

items.label: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 2.0d uses Ford Duratorq codes FMBA, FMBB or AJ20D4. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.
items.value: 6. Can I repair the 2.0d's EGR system, or does it need a new engine?

items.label: Yes — EGR cleaning or replacement is a straightforward repair costing £100–£400. The EGR downpipe is also prone to splitting and can be replaced. Neither issue typically requires engine replacement. Regular cleaning is the best prevention.
